function.name: apply_patch
function.description:
Apply a patch to create, update, or delete files using the unified patch format. This uses a 6-pass fuzzy matching engine with Levenshtein distance matching and automatic indentation handling, plus a loose fallback matcher for imperfect GPT patches.

Patch format:
```
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /absolute/path/to/file.ext
@@ optional_context_hint @@
 context line (unchanged)
-removed line
+added line
 context line (unchanged)
*** End Patch
```

Supported operations:
- `*** Update File: <path>` — Edit an existing file using context-based hunks
- `*** Add File: <path>` — Create a new file (all lines prefixed with `+`)
- `*** Delete File: <path>` — Delete an existing file
- `*** Move to: <new_path>` — (after Update File) Move/rename the file

Hunk rules:
- Each hunk starts with `@@` (optionally followed by a context hint line from the file)
- Lines starting with ` ` (space) are context (must exist in file, used for matching)
- Lines starting with `-` are deleted
- Lines starting with `+` are inserted
- Include 1–3 lines of context before and after changes for accurate matching
- Multiple hunks per file are separated by `@@`
- `*** End of File` can mark the final hunk at file end

Multiple files can be modified in a single patch by listing multiple file operations between `*** Begin Patch` and `*** End Patch`.

IMPORTANT:
- Use absolute paths whenever possible (relative paths are resolved against the current working directory)
- Context lines should match the actual file content (whitespace-tolerant)
- Provide enough unique context so the engine can locate the edit site
- For large files, include at least 2-3 context lines around each change
- The engine auto-detects and converts indentation (tabs ↔ spaces)

input.description: The full patch text, starting with '*** Begin Patch' and ending with '*** End Patch'. Contains one or more file operations (Add/Update/Delete) with context-based hunks.

explanation.description: Short intent summary used for healing or debugging if the patch fails to apply.

commonErrors.solution: The context lines in the patch don't match the actual file content. Re-read the file and provide accurate context lines.
The file specified in *** Update File or *** Delete File does not exist. Use *** Add File to create new files.
The patch is malformed — ensure it ends with '*** End Patch'.
The same file path appears more than once in the patch. Combine all edits for a file under a single *** Update File block.
